title: Ideal Beauty — 阅读策略训练
subject: 英语
teachingNotes: 高一英语阅读策略训练课，围绕 B7U2 Ideal Beauty 课文，通过 5 步阅读法（Predict → Skim → Scan+Matrix → Evaluate → Recap）引导学生从信息提取到批判性思维。三端联动教学：教师控制台推进课堂节奏，学生端完成任务，投屏黑板展示结构化板书。

paragraphs.text: In many parts of Nigeria, it is traditional for women to go to special "fattening rooms" before they get married. Happiness Edem was one such young woman, and she went from 60 kg to twice that weight because, in her culture, being fat is a sign of wealth.
But what about the rest of the world? Open any fashion magazine and you will see young models — women who are slim and fair. Many people are worried that modern media promotes shallow beauty ideals.

Ideas about physical beauty change over time and different periods of history have had their own idea of what is beautiful. In ancient Egypt, both men and women used to paint dark kohl around their eyes.
In the 1600s in Europe, being plump and pale-skinned was considered stunning beauty. Rubens painted many women with round, soft bodies. In Elizabethan England, pale skin was a sign of wealth.
Within different cultures around the world, we can find diverse ideas about physical beauty. In Borneo, many people have tattoos. For them, their body art is like a diary of important events.
In New Zealand, the Maori people have their own tradition — a form of tattooing called tā moko. Unlike in Borneo, these tattoos show a person's position in society.

European visitors to Myanmar were amazed to see women wearing metal rings around their necks. And in Indonesia, some people practised sharpening their teeth, as it was considered a form of cultural identity.
It appears that people change their appearance to tell the world about their culture and status. Whether kohl, tattoos, or metal rings, these practices reflect cultural values, not just vanity.

aiReferenceQA.a: Skimming 是一种快速阅读策略，指通过浏览标题、首句和关键词来获取文章大意，而不需要逐字阅读。
信号词（signal words）帮助你判断段落的功能和结构。比如 'however' 表示转折，'for example' 表示举例，它们是理解文章逻辑的路标。
根据课文 ¶1，在尼日利亚，肥胖被视为财富和地位的象征。文中提到 'being fat is a sign of wealth.'
想想看：文章从 ¶1 开始引出话题，然后每个段落讲一个不同国家的例子。你能找到哪些段落在讲不同国家吗？试着把它们分组。
Evaluating 是批判性思考策略。读完一段后，问自己：我同意作者的观点吗？作者的证据充分吗？这和我知道的是否一致？

readingSteps.label: 课前导读
readingSteps.description: 课前导读：了解本节课的学习目标和阅读方法。

studentView.title: 欢迎来到阅读策略课
studentView.body:
今天我们要学习一篇关于世界各地不同审美观的英语文章 —— Ideal Beauty。

你将学到 4 种阅读策略，帮助你快速、高效地读懂任何说明文或议论文：

  1. Predicting（预测）—— 看标题，激活已有知识
  2. Skimming（略读）—— 只读首句，抓文章骨架
  3. Scanning（寻读）—— 带着问题细读，提取关键信息
  4. Evaluating（评价）—— 用证据批判性思考

准备好了吗？让我们开始！
keyPoints: 本节课共 5 个任务，每个任务前有方法讲解
不需要逐字读完全文——策略帮你用更少的时间获取更多信息
遇到生词不要怕，关注信号词和结构比词汇更重要

teacherView.speechLine: 学生正在阅读课前导读

cueCards.title: 导读说明
cueCards.body:
学生正在自主阅读课前导读，了解本节课的四种阅读策略。
无需干预，等待学生点击「我准备好了」后自动进入 Step 1。

readingSteps.label: 图式激活
readingSteps.description: 引导学生关注标题 Ideal Beauty，快速提取 ¶1-2 的核心冲突：尼日利亚增肥室 vs 现代媒体审美。

probe.q: For the last question — how did you figure out the answer? Where exactly in the text did you find the clue?
probe.translate: 你是怎么看出作者的核心问题的？从文章哪里找到的？

discuss.targetInsight: ¶2 uses the phrase 'Many people are worried' to introduce the concept of 'shallow beauty ideals' as a concern, not the author's own conclusion. The conflict opening (¶1 Nigeria vs ¶2 media) sets up the central question.
commonMisconceptions: Confusing Happiness Edem's story with the author's core question
Thinking ¶2's last sentence is a statement rather than an implied question
Only seeing the Nigeria vs media surface contrast without noticing the deeper universality question
scaffoldStrategies: If vague: 'Which specific sentence in ¶2 gave you the clue?'
If only mentions ¶1: 'Good — now look at how ¶2 ends. What question is the writer really asking?'
If correct: 'Notice the writer's technique: two opposing examples → big question. This is called a conflict opening.'
discuss.insight: Key technique: The text uses a "conflict opening" — two opposing facts, then a question. This is common in argumentative writing.
discuss.insightZh: 文章用"冲突开头"——先给两个对立事实，再提出问题。这是议论文常见写法。

teacherView.speechLine: Look at the title — **Ideal Beauty**. Before reading, what comes to mind?
teacherView.focusSubtitle: ¶1-2 · 尼日利亚 vs 现代媒体

quickActions.label: 标题联想
quickActions.message: 看标题 Ideal Beauty，你脑海里想到什么？试着说出 3 个关键词。

quickActions.label: 读¶1尼日利亚
quickActions.message: 请阅读第1段：在尼日利亚，结婚前要去 fattening rooms — 胖=富有。这和你的认知一样吗？

quickActions.label: 对比提示
quickActions.message: ¶1 说胖是美，¶2 说 slim and fair 才是美 — 谁对？带着这个问题继续读。

quickActions.label: 再给 2 分钟
quickActions.message: 再给大家 2 分钟时间完成当前任务

cueCards.title: 激活策略
cueCards.body:
不要急着读全文。先让学生看标题 + ¶1，形成「预期」。

提问模板：
• "What do you think 'ideal beauty' means?"
• "Is there only ONE definition?"

cueCards.title: ¶1-2 要点
cueCards.body:
¶1: Nigeria — fattening rooms — 60→120kg — fat = wealth
¶2: fashion magazines — slim & fair — shallow beauty ideals

核心冲突：文化多样性 vs 媒体单一审美

cueCards.title: 过渡到 Step 2
cueCards.body:
当多数学生能说出「不同文化有不同审美」时，引导过渡：
"Good. Now let's see HOW the author organizes these ideas. Read only the first sentence of each paragraph."

readingSteps.label: Skimming 讲解
readingSteps.description: 讲解 Skimming 阅读策略：只读首句 + 关键转折词，快速把握文章骨架。

studentView.title: 什么是 Skimming（略读）？
studentView.body:
Skimming 是一种快速阅读技巧：不逐字读，只读每段的第一句话和转折词（signal words）。

为什么有用？因为英语说明文/议论文有一个规律：每段的第一句通常是主题句（topic sentence），告诉你这一段在讲什么。

你需要找的信号词包括：

  • change over time → 说明在讲时间变化（历史）
  • around the world / different cultures → 说明在讲地理差异（文化）
  • It appears that → 说明是总结 / 结论

接下来的任务中，你要用 Skimming 方法读 ¶3-8 的首句，判断每段属于哪个结构板块。
keyPoints: 核心公式：首句 + 信号词 = 段落功能
3 分钟 Skimming > 30 分钟逐字读
找到信号词后，判断段落属于 History / Culture / Conclusion 哪一类

teacherView.speechLine: 学生正在学习 Skimming 方法

cueCards.title: 自学内容
cueCards.body:
学生正在阅读 Skimming 方法讲解：首句 + 信号词 = 段落功能。
等待学生确认后进入结构解码任务。

readingSteps.label: 结构解码
readingSteps.description: 只读 ¶3-8 首句 + 关键转折词，提炼文章骨架：Phenomenon → History → Culture → Conclusion。

probe.q: How did you decide that ¶3-4 is about "History" and not "Culture"? What signal words helped you?
probe.translate: 你怎么判断 ¶3-4 是"历史"而不是"文化"？哪些信号词帮了你？

discuss.targetInsight: ¶3 first sentence contains 'change over time' and 'different periods of history' — TIME signal words. ¶5 contains 'different cultures around the world' — PLACE signal words. The distinction is time vs space.
commonMisconceptions: Confusing History and Culture because both involve different countries
Not recognizing 'change over time' as a time signal vs 'around the world' as a place signal
Thinking ¶8 is another culture paragraph rather than a conclusion
scaffoldStrategies: If confused: 'Read just the first sentence of ¶3. What words tell you about TIME?'
If can't distinguish: 'Compare ¶3 first sentence with ¶5 first sentence. One talks about time, one about place — which is which?'
If correct: 'Great! You found the key: time words = History, place words = Culture. This pattern works for any text.'
discuss.insight: Skimming: Read first sentences + signal words. Time words → History. Place words → Culture. Summary words → Conclusion. Full structure in 3 minutes.
discuss.insightZh: 略读：读首句 + 信号词。时间词→历史，地理词→文化，总结词→结论。

teacherView.speechLine: Now read only the **first sentence** of each paragraph. Find the **signal words**.
teacherView.focusSubtitle: 首句 + 转折词 → 四段结构

quickActions.label: 只读首句！
quickActions.message: 注意！只读每段的第一句话，不要读全段。找出关键转折词。

quickActions.label: 信号词提示
quickActions.message: 找这些词：change over time, different cultures, around the world, It appears that — 它们告诉你段落的功能。

quickActions.label: 文章结构
quickActions.message: 文章骨架：¶1-2 现象 → ¶3-4 历史 → ¶5-7 文化 → ¶8 结论。你找到了吗？

quickActions.label: 再给 2 分钟
quickActions.message: 再给大家 2 分钟时间完成当前任务

cueCards.title: Skimming 方法
cueCards.body:
核心公式：首句 + 信号词 = 段落功能

不要逐字读！3 分钟拿到全文骨架 vs 30 分钟逐字读。
提问："What does 'change over time' tell you about ¶3?"

cueCards.title: 四段结构
cueCards.body:
¶1-2: Phenomenon — 现象对比（Nigeria vs Media）
¶3-4: History — 跨时间（Egypt, 1600s Europe）
¶5-7: Culture — 跨空间（Borneo, Maori, Myanmar, Indonesia）
¶8: Conclusion — 总结（tell the world）

cueCards.title: 过渡到 Step 3
cueCards.body:
确认学生找到四段结构后：
"Good, we now have the skeleton. Let's fill in the flesh — each group focuses on 2-3 paragraphs and builds a matrix."

readingSteps.label: Scanning 讲解
readingSteps.description: 讲解 Scanning 阅读策略：带着具体问题细读目标段落，提取关键信息填入矩阵表。

studentView.title: 什么是 Scanning（寻读）？
studentView.body:
Scanning 和 Skimming 不同 —— 这次你要带着问题细读，在文章中快速定位具体信息。

想象你在字典里查一个单词：你不会从头到尾读字典，而是目标明确地扫描，找到那个词就停下来。Scanning 就是这个道理。

接下来你要构建一个 Place × Practice × Reason 矩阵表：

  • Place：哪个国家/文化？
  • Practice：他们做什么来追求美？
  • Reason：为什么这样做？（文化意义是什么？）

我已经帮你填好了示范行（Ancient Egypt）。你的任务是用 Scanning 方法从 ¶5-7 中找出其余 4 个地方的信息。
keyPoints: Scanning = 带着问题读，目标明确
每一行都要回答三个问题：在哪里？做什么？为什么？
注意：tā moko ≠ tattoo，要写准确名称
¶7 包含两个不同的地方（Myanmar 和 Indonesia），要分两行写

teacherView.speechLine: 学生正在学习 Scanning 方法

cueCards.title: 自学内容
cueCards.body:
学生正在阅读 Scanning 方法讲解：带着 Place × Practice × Reason 问题细读。
等待学生确认后进入矩阵构建任务。

readingSteps.label: 矩阵构建
readingSteps.description: 分组寻读：A组找历史证据(¶3-4)，B组找文化证据(¶5-7)。合作完成 Place × Practice × Reason 矩阵表。

probe.q: Look at your completed matrix. Are these beauty practices only about "looking good"? What do they have in common?
probe.translate: 这些审美实践仅仅是为了好看吗？有什么共同点？

discuss.targetInsight: Every beauty practice in the matrix carries deeper cultural meaning: wealth (Egypt, Europe), life records (Borneo), social position (Maori), cultural identity (Indonesia). The common thread is that beauty = cultural language for expressing identity and status.
commonMisconceptions: Thinking beauty practices are just about appearance/vanity
Not seeing the common pattern across different cultures
Only describing what each culture does without explaining WHY
scaffoldStrategies: If surface-level: 'Look at the Reason column. What pattern do you see across all the rows?'
If only lists facts: 'Good facts! But what do wealth, diary, social position, and cultural identity have in common?'
If sees the pattern: 'Exactly — beauty is a cultural language. Now think about what reading method helped you find all this.'
discuss.insight: Beauty practices are more than "looking good" — they're about identity, status, and culture. Scanning helps you extract organized evidence from long texts.
discuss.insightZh: 审美实践不只是好看——背后是身份、地位、文化。Scanning 帮你从长文中提取结构化信息。

teacherView.speechLine: Good, we now have the **skeleton**. Let's fill in the **flesh** — **Place × Practice × Reason**.

quickActions.label: Myanmar 位置提示
quickActions.message: Myanmar (¶7a): 缅甸的美容实践是长颈族用铜环拉长脖子，象征美丽和身份

quickActions.label: Practice 写法示例
quickActions.message: Practice 列要写具体做法，例如 "kohl eye makeup" 而不是 "makeup"

quickActions.label: tā moko 生词卡
quickActions.message: tā moko: 毛利人的传统面部纹身，表示社会地位和家族身份

quickActions.label: 再给 2 分钟
quickActions.message: 再给大家 2 分钟时间完成当前任务

cueCards.title: 示范一行
cueCards.body:
用 Ancient Egypt (¶3) 打样：
• "What did Egyptians do?" → kohl makeup
• "Why?" → wealth & status

让学生模仿这个模式填其他行。

cueCards.title: 易错点
cueCards.body:
¶6 Maori: Practice 写 "tattoos" 过泛 → 应写 tā moko；Reason 是 social position。

¶7: Myanmar 与 Indonesia 是两行不同地方，常被合并成一行。提醒分开写。

cueCards.title: 过渡到 Step 4
cueCards.body:
回到矩阵整体，指向 Reason 列：
"Look at the Reason column. What do you see? Are these really 'shallow beauty ideals'?"

readingSteps.label: 批判性阅读讲解
readingSteps.description: 讲解批判性阅读方法：区分事实与观点，用证据支持自己的立场。

studentView.title: 如何批判性地阅读？
studentView.body:
前面你已经用 Skimming 抓住了文章骨架，用 Scanning 提取了具体信息。现在到了最关键的一步 —— Evaluating（评价）。

回到 ¶2，作者写道：

"Many people are worried that modern media promotes shallow beauty ideals."

注意：这是 "many people" 的看法，不一定是作者的结论。

你的任务是用矩阵表中的事实证据来判断：这些美的实践真的是 "shallow（肤浅的）" 吗？

写议论段的模板：

I [agree/disagree] that beauty ideals are shallow.
First, in [Place], [Practice] because [Reason].
Second, in [Place], [Practice] because [Reason].
Therefore, beauty is [not] shallow.
keyPoints: 区分观点和事实："many people are worried" 是观点，不是定论
用你矩阵表中的至少 2 条证据支持你的立场
用 First / Second / Therefore 组织段落结构
没有标准答案 —— agree、disagree、partly agree 都可以，关键是有证据

teacherView.speechLine: 学生正在学习批判性阅读方法

cueCards.title: 自学内容
cueCards.body:
学生正在阅读批判性阅读讲解：区分事实与观点，用矩阵证据写议论段。
等待学生确认后进入批判质疑任务。

readingSteps.label: 批判质疑
readingSteps.description: 利用矩阵证据挑战 ¶2 的 'shallow beauty ideals'，写一段议论段。

probe.q: Pick your strongest piece of evidence. Explain: what does it prove about beauty standards?
probe.translate: 挑最有说服力的一条证据，解释它能证明什么。

discuss.targetInsight: Strong arguments use the claim-evidence-explanation chain. The evidence from the matrix (e.g., 1600s plump = beautiful proves standards change over time) shows that calling any standard 'the only right one' is shallow. ¶8's conclusion supports this.
commonMisconceptions: Stating evidence without explaining what it proves
Not connecting matrix evidence to the 'shallow' claim in ¶2
scaffoldStrategies: If no explanation: 'You picked good evidence. Now tell me: what does this PROVE about beauty standards?'
If confused about writer's view: 'Look at ¶8. Does the writer think beauty is shallow or cultural?'
If strong argument: 'Great chain: position → evidence → explanation. This is academic writing.'
discuss.insight: Strong opinions need evidence: 1) State position. 2) Give evidence. 3) Explain what it proves. This is the "claim → evidence → explanation" chain.
discuss.insightZh: 好观点需要证据：1) 表明立场；2) 给证据；3) 解释证据证明了什么。

teacherView.speechLine: Look at the **Reason column** in your matrix. Are these really **shallow beauty ideals**?
teacherView.focusTitle: 评分标准 & 写作模板
teacherView.focusSubtitle: 议论段：立场 + 矩阵证据

quickActions.label: 回顾 Reason 列
quickActions.message: 回看你的矩阵 Reason 列：wealth, diary, social position, cultural identity — 这些算 shallow 吗？

quickActions.label: 引用矩阵证据
quickActions.message: 写议论段时，至少引用矩阵中 2 个地方的证据。例如：In Borneo... In New Zealand...

quickActions.label: 议论段模板
quickActions.message: 模板：I disagree that beauty ideals are shallow. First, in [Place], [Practice] because [Reason]. Second, in [Place]... Therefore, beauty is not shallow.

quickActions.label: 再给 2 分钟
quickActions.message: 再给大家 2 分钟时间完成当前任务

cueCards.title: 引导批判思维
cueCards.body:
¶2 说 "shallow beauty ideals" — 但这是 many people 的看法，不是作者的结论。

提问："Does the author agree? What evidence does he give?"
学生需要从矩阵的 Reason 列找反驳证据。

cueCards.title: 评分标准
cueCards.body:
三个评分点：
1. 立场清晰（agree / disagree / partly agree）
2. 引用 ≥ 2 条矩阵事实作为证据
3. 用 First / Second / Therefore 组织段落结构

cueCards.title: 过渡到 Step 5
cueCards.body:
选 1-2 篇好的学生作品展示，然后引导：
"Great arguments. Now let's step back — what reading strategies did we use today?"

readingSteps.label: 策略回顾指引
readingSteps.description: 引导学生回顾本节课学到的 4 种阅读策略，为复盘做准备。

studentView.title: 回顾：你今天学了什么？
studentView.body:
恭喜你完成了所有阅读任务！现在让我们回顾一下今天用到的 4 种阅读策略：

Step 1Predicting — 看标题，激活你已有的知识
Step 2Skimming — 读首句 + 信号词，抓文章骨架
Step 3Scanning — 带着问题细读，提取信息填矩阵
Step 4Evaluating — 用证据批判性思考，写议论段

这套方法适用于任何说明文和议论文。下次考试阅读理解时，试试用这 4 步法！
keyPoints: 4 步法的顺序很重要：先预测 → 再略读 → 再细读 → 最后评价
文章的主旨在 ¶8：美不是肤浅的虚荣，而是文化的语言
回家作业：用同样的 4 步法阅读 "Beyond the Plate"

teacherView.speechLine: 学生正在回顾阅读策略

cueCards.title: 自学内容
cueCards.body:
学生正在回顾 4 种阅读策略的总结。
等待学生确认后进入最终复盘步骤。

readingSteps.label: 复盘升华
readingSteps.description: 回顾 4 步阅读策略：Predict → Skim → Scan+Matrix → Evaluate。布置作业。

probe.q: If you get a new article next time — "Beyond the Plate" — what would you do first? Why?
probe.translate: 如果下次给你新文章，你会先做什么？为什么？

discuss.targetInsight: The 4-step reading process (Predict → Skim → Scan → Evaluate) works for any argumentative/expository text. The order matters: big picture first, then details, then critical thinking.
commonMisconceptions: Jumping straight to reading word-by-word instead of predicting first
Confusing the order of Skim and Scan
Not seeing that these strategies form a reusable process
scaffoldStrategies: If wrong order: 'Think about what makes sense: would you look for details BEFORE knowing the structure?'
If only names strategies: 'Good! But WHY does Predict come first? What does it give you?'
If correct: 'Perfect process! Now which strategy helped YOU the most today?'
discuss.insight: Today: not just "beauty" but a reading process — Predict → Skim → Scan → Evaluate. Works for any argumentative text.
discuss.insightZh: 今天学的不只是"美"，更是阅读方法：Predict → Skim → Scan → Evaluate。

teacherView.speechLine: Let's recap. We used **4 strategies** today: Predicting, Skimming, Scanning, and Evaluating.

quickActions.label: Predict 回顾
quickActions.message: Step 1 我们用了 Predicting：看标题 + ¶1，预测文章讲什么。这帮你准备好了阅读方向。

quickActions.label: Skim 回顾
quickActions.message: Step 2 我们用了 Skimming：只读首句 + 信号词，3 分钟拿到文章骨架。

quickActions.label: 布置作业
quickActions.message: 作业：用今天学的 4 步法阅读 "Beyond the Plate"。提交 structure map + matrix。下节课对比讲评。

quickActions.label: 再给 2 分钟
quickActions.message: 再给大家 2 分钟时间完成当前任务

cueCards.title: 策略总结
cueCards.body:
今天的 4 步阅读法：
1. Predict — 看标题，激活已知
2. Skim — 读首句，抓骨架
3. Scan — 细读填矩阵，提取证据
4. Evaluate — 用证据批判，写议论段

这套方法适用于任何说明文/议论文。

cueCards.title: ¶8 主旨
cueCards.body:
"It appears that people change their appearance to tell the world about their culture and status."

核心观点：美不是 shallow vanity，而是 cultural language — 表达身份、归属、地位。

cueCards.title: 作业说明
cueCards.body:
课后阅读 "Beyond the Plate"：
1. 用 4 步法自主阅读
2. 画 structure map（文章骨架图）
3. 填 matrix 表
4. 下节课对比讲评

提交到学生端即可。

lesson.title: Ideal Beauty
lesson.subtitle: 高一(3)班 · 阅读策略训练

data.text: Many people are worried that modern media promotes shallow beauty ideals.
作者真的同意 "shallow" 吗？带着这个怀疑读下去。
找证据：每个文化都有自己的"理想"，这算 shallow 吗？
每段只读首句 + 转折词 = 拿到全文骨架。3 分钟 vs 30 分钟。

data.expr: 首句 + [change / within / It appears] = 段落功能
data.caption: 这是 skimming 的核心公式
答题句型 — 用矩阵每一行套这句话

data.author: 林雅婷
data.text: In Borneo, people get tattoos because they record important life events — like a diary on skin.

data.author: 陈思远
data.text: In Myanmar, women wear neck rings because they look beautiful.
思远 → 雅婷：从 "好看" 到 "文化意义"。回看 ¶8 "tell the world about culture and status"
作者只是引用了 "many people" 的看法 — 这是一个观点，不是结论。

branches.label: Partly agree
leaves: BUT: 不同文化都有 "理想"

data.text:
评分点：
1. 立场清晰
2. 引用 >= 2 条矩阵事实
3. 用 First / Second / Therefore 组段

data.expr: I [agree/disagree] that ... First, in [Place]... Second, in [Place]... Therefore, beauty is [not] shallow.
data.caption: 议论段模板

data.text: It appears that people change their appearance to tell the world about their culture and status.
HW：用今天的 4 步法读 "Beyond the Plate"。提交 structure map + matrix。下节课对比讲评。

observationAnchors.label: Scanning 策略识别
observationAnchors.description: 能区分 scanning 和精读，快速定位关键信息

observationAnchors.label: 文本结构理解
observationAnchors.description: 识别不同文化/时代的审美标准对比结构（Phenomenon→History→Culture→Conclusion）

observationAnchors.label: 细节信息提取
observationAnchors.description: 从指定段落准确提取事实信息（地点、做法、原因）

observationAnchors.label: 批判性立场表达
observationAnchors.description: 能形成有理有据的个人立场并引用文本证据

observationAnchors.label: 信息排序逻辑
observationAnchors.description: 理解文章信息的逻辑顺序和时间顺序，能正确排列阅读策略步骤

observationAnchors.label: 表面理解陷阱
observationAnchors.description: 只理解字面意思，不理解深层含义（如把 standard of beauty 理解为「标准美」而非「审美标准」）

observationAnchors.label: 证据不足论证
observationAnchors.description: 表达立场时不引用文本证据，只给个人感受

observationAnchors.label: 细节混淆
observationAnchors.description: 混淆不同文化/段落的细节信息（如把 A 文化的做法归给 B 文化）

observationAnchors.label: 策略泛化误用
observationAnchors.description: 把 scanning 当成精读，或者不理解不同阅读策略的适用场景
